rationale: Hard pass for Helix Jump. The archetype's muted earth-tone palette (dark slate, saddle brown, olive) and detailed 2D illustrative cross-section style are the polar opposite of the game's neon high-contrast 3D minimalism, and there's no narrative/character layer in Helix Jump to justify a curiosity-driven storytelling hook. The only weak bridge is a vertical descending camera motion (bunker levels ≈ helix levels), but forcing that adaptation would misrepresent the actual gameplay and likely drive low IPM despite broad audience overlap. Skip unless we test a heavily reworked "descend through layers" hybrid that swaps the art back to neon helix visuals.
